flipABoatCodes.title: Flip A Boat Codes
flipABoatCodes.subtitle: Redeem the latest Flip A Boat code for free Cash to fund your first boat purchase and required repairs.
flipABoatCodes.intro: Flip A Boat codes are redeemed through the Settings menu. Enter codes exactly as displayed and use Cash rewards as a repair reserve instead of spending everything on a larger project boat.

codes.note: Use the reward for a manageable boat and the replacement parts required to complete its repair.

expired.message: No expired Flip A Boat codes are currently listed.

flipABoatCodes.redeemTitle: How to Redeem Flip A Boat Codes
redeemSteps: Launch Flip A Boat through its official Roblox page.
Select the gear button in the bottom-left corner.
Scroll to the Redeem Code section in Settings.
Enter release in the code box.
Press Enter or Return to claim 10,000 Cash.

flipABoatBeginnerGuide.title: Flip A Boat Beginner Guide
flipABoatBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Buy a damaged boat, haul it to your workspace, repair it part by part and sell it for more Cash.
flipABoatBeginnerGuide.intro: The main progression loop begins with a beat-up 11-foot boat and eventually reaches large 40-foot center-console boats with four outboard engines. Successful beginners protect their repair budget, complete required mechanical work first, and reinvest each sale into the next project.

steps.title: Claim Your Launch Cash
steps.description: Redeem the code release for 10,000 Cash before purchasing your first project.
steps.tip: Keep most of the reward available for replacement parts.
steps.mistake: Spending the entire reward on the purchase price and having no Cash left for repairs.

steps.title: Buy a Finishable Boat
steps.description: Start with the lowest progression tier, including the 11-foot starter project, and review its damaged components before committing.
steps.tip: Choose a boat that leaves a clear repair reserve after purchase.
steps.mistake: Buying the largest affordable hull instead of the boat you can fully repair.

steps.title: Haul It to the Workshop
steps.description: Use the truck and boat trailer to collect the project, align the trailer carefully, and transport the boat to your repair area.
steps.tip: Use the transport equipment already available before spending on optional upgrades.
steps.mistake: Approaching the trailer too quickly and repeatedly missing the attachment position.

steps.title: Inspect Every Damaged Part
steps.description: Open the boat's repair interactions and check the hull, outboard engine, steering controls, gauges, and other highlighted components.
steps.tip: Buy only the exact parts requested by the repair interface.
steps.mistake: Purchasing unrelated parts or cosmetic items before completing the inspection.

steps.title: Repair Before Customizing
steps.description: Complete required mechanical and structural repairs first. Paint and optional customization should come after the boat's required condition work is finished.
steps.tip: Prioritize engines, steering, gauges, electronics, and required hull components.
steps.mistake: Using starting Cash on paint or accessories while important systems remain damaged.

steps.title: Sell and Reinvest
steps.description: Complete a final condition check, sell the restored boat for Cash, and use the profit to purchase a larger project. Boats can also be customized and stored as part of your fleet.
steps.tip: Move up one progression tier at a time and keep a repair reserve for every new project.
steps.mistake: Treating the sale price as pure profit without subtracting the boat and part costs.

flipABoatBoatsAndProgression.title: Flip A Boat Boats and Progression
flipABoatBoatsAndProgression.subtitle: Progress from an 11-foot starter project to a 40-foot center console powered by four outboard engines.
flipABoatBoatsAndProgression.intro: Boat purchase prices are displayed in the live market and scale with vessel size, condition, and repair workload. Larger projects require more working capital and parts, but they also provide greater resale potential and progression value.

boats.strategy: Complete several low-cost flips and preserve Cash for required parts.
Choose projects with short repair lists and repeat the full buy-repair-sell loop.
Track total part spending and avoid optional customization that removes the sale margin.
Enter this tier only when the purchase price and full repair reserve can both be covered.
Use established profits and rebirth bonuses to fund the purchase and four-engine workload.

flipABoatRepairGuide.title: Flip A Boat Repair Guide
flipABoatRepairGuide.subtitle: Inspect the project, replace every required component, and finish the mechanical condition pass before spending on paint.
flipABoatRepairGuide.intro: Flip A Boat uses a part-by-part restoration system. Repair costs are shown when purchasing each required component and increase with the boat's size, condition, and number of outboard engines.

steps.title: Position the Boat
steps.action: Haul the damaged boat with the truck and trailer, park it inside the repair area, and confirm that its repair interactions are available.
steps.tool: Truck, boat trailer, and workshop interaction
steps.location: Trailer connection and workshop parking area
steps.check: The boat can be inspected and its damaged components can be selected.

steps.title: Complete a Full Inspection
steps.action: Move around the hull and helm, interact with highlighted repair points, and record every required replacement before buying parts.
steps.tool: Boat repair interface and parts inventory
steps.location: Hull, deck, stern, helm, and interior
steps.cost: Displayed separately for every requested part
steps.check: Every highlighted repair point has been reviewed.

steps.title: Repair the Outboard Engines
steps.action: Replace the requested engine components at the stern. Larger boats can carry multiple outboards, with the 40-foot center console using four.
steps.tool: Engine repair interaction and motor parts
steps.cost: Scales with the engine setup and number of outboards
steps.check: No required engine interaction remains highlighted.

steps.title: Restore Steering and Gauges
steps.action: Replace requested helm components such as the steering wheel, gauges, and connected electrical equipment.
steps.cost: Shown when each helm component is selected
steps.check: Steering and instrument repair points are complete.

steps.title: Finish Hull and Interior Parts
steps.action: Complete all remaining structural, deck, seating, and cosmetic component requests shown on the boat.
steps.priority: Complete required parts before optional upgrades
steps.tool: Hull, deck, and interior replacement parts
steps.location: Outer hull, deck, and passenger area
steps.cost: Depends on the number and type of damaged components
steps.check: No required part remains missing or damaged.

steps.title: Customize the Finished Boat
steps.action: Apply paint and optional customization only after all required repair work has been completed.
steps.cost: Optional spending added to the total project cost
steps.check: Customization remains within the expected resale margin.

steps.title: Run the Pre-Sale Check
steps.action: Inspect the entire boat again, confirm every highlighted component is complete, and compare the purchase plus repair total with the offered sale value.
steps.cost: Boat price plus all required and optional part spending
steps.check: All repairs are complete and the sale produces a positive margin.

flipABoatMoneyGuide.title: Flip A Boat Money Guide
flipABoatMoneyGuide.subtitle: Build profit by choosing a project you can finish, controlling repair spending, and selling before optional upgrades erase the margin.
flipABoatMoneyGuide.intro: Flip A Boat is built around a simple business loop: buy a broken boat, haul it home, repair it part by part, and sell it for more than the total project cost. The safest route is to keep a repair reserve, complete required work first, and move into larger hulls only after smaller flips can fund the full purchase-and-repair cycle.

strategies.title: Beginner: Protect Starting Cash
strategies.strategy: Choose the smallest project you can buy and fully repair without spending every dollar. Finish the required parts, sell the completed boat, and reinvest the return into the next flip.
strategies.profitRule: Keep cash available for parts and hauling until the boat is ready to sell.
strategies.avoid: Do not spend the repair reserve on paint or optional customization before the mechanical work is complete.

strategies.title: Mid-Game: Repeat Profitable Sizes
strategies.stage: Established small and medium boat flips
strategies.strategy: Repeat boat sizes that you can transport and repair quickly. Track the purchase price and every part cost, then compare that total with the final sale value before moving to a larger project.
strategies.profitRule: Scale up only when one completed sale can cover the next boat and its repair reserve.
strategies.avoid: Do not jump to a larger hull when the purchase leaves too little cash to finish it.

strategies.title: Experienced: High-Capital Projects
strategies.stage: Up to a 40-foot center console with quad outboards
strategies.strategy: Use accumulated cash for larger boats with more parts, more hauling work, and a longer restoration cycle. Control optional spending and complete the functional build before deciding whether extra customization belongs on the resale project or in your personal fleet.
strategies.profitRule: Measure profit after purchase, parts, and optional upgrades—not from the sale price alone.
strategies.avoid: Do not let a large unfinished project lock all available cash.

strategies.title: Fast Profit Checklist
strategies.strategy: Buy below your total budget, reserve money for repairs, haul the boat carefully, complete required systems, limit cosmetic spending, sell, and reinvest.
strategies.profitRule: Profit equals sale value minus boat purchase and all repair or customization costs.
strategies.avoid: Do not compare boats by sale value alone; the best flip is the one with a strong margin and a short completion time.

flipABoatCustomizationAndFleet.title: Flip A Boat Customization and Fleet Guide
flipABoatCustomizationAndFleet.subtitle: Separate visual changes from functional boat parts so each project has a clear purpose: quick resale, performance build, or permanent fleet boat.
flipABoatCustomizationAndFleet.intro: Completed boats can be painted, customized, sold, or stored in a personal fleet. Cosmetic work changes the look of a boat, while repaired parts and outboard configurations determine whether the boat is complete and how it behaves on the water. Finish the functional restoration first, then decide how much of the remaining budget belongs in appearance upgrades.

features.title: Paint and Exterior Style
features.effect: Changes the boat's appearance and lets each restoration use a distinct color scheme.
features.bestUse: Apply paint after required repairs so the project budget remains focused on finishing the boat.
features.priority: Use restrained cosmetic spending on resale boats and save elaborate themes for boats you plan to keep.

features.title: Visual Customization
features.effect: Personalizes the finished boat without replacing the part-by-part repair process.
features.bestUse: Create a consistent hull, trim, and equipment look once the mechanical condition is complete.
features.priority: Treat every visual add-on as an optional cost and compare it with the final sale offer.

features.title: Outboard Configuration
features.effect: Outboards are part of the working boat setup and affect how the boat is configured for the water. Endgame progression reaches a 40-foot center console with quad outboards.
features.bestUse: Complete the required engine and outboard work before spending on appearance upgrades.
features.priority: Functional completion takes priority because an unfinished propulsion setup prevents the boat from becoming a clean flip.

features.title: Personal Fleet Storage
features.effect: Finished boats can be kept and stored instead of sold, allowing players to build a personal fleet.
features.bestUse: Keep distinctive builds, favorite hulls, or boats you want to use on the game's realistic waves and boat physics.
features.priority: A fleet boat is a long-term ownership choice; a resale boat should stay focused on margin and completion speed.

flipABoatTrucksAndTrailers.title: Flip A Boat Trucks and Trailers Guide
flipABoatTrucksAndTrailers.subtitle: A clean haul protects the boat, trailer, and profit margin. Use slow alignment and smooth driving, especially as hull size increases.
flipABoatTrucksAndTrailers.intro: Truck and trailer hauling connects every stage of a Flip A Boat project. The reliable method is to line up straight, attach through the on-screen interaction, secure the boat before moving, and use small steering inputs around the workshop and launch ramp. Larger boats need more room and slower corrections because the game uses realistic boat and wave physics.

steps.title: Choose the Hauling Setup
steps.instruction: Use a truck-and-trailer setup that can position the current boat without the hull hanging far off the trailer.
steps.largeBoatTip: Give larger hulls more turning room and plan the route before attaching.
steps.problem: The boat and trailer do not line up.
steps.solution: Move back to level ground, straighten both vehicles, and approach again at low speed.

steps.title: Align the Hitch
steps.instruction: Back the truck toward the trailer in a straight line and use small steering corrections until the attachment prompt is available.
steps.largeBoatTip: Avoid sharp angles that make the trailer swing before the hitch connects.
steps.solution: Pull forward, center the truck with the trailer tongue, and retry from a straighter angle.

steps.title: Load and Secure the Boat
steps.instruction: Center the hull on the trailer, move slowly until it is seated, and complete the available securing interaction before towing.
steps.largeBoatTip: Check that the bow and stern are centered before moving a long hull.
steps.problem: The boat shifts, leans, or falls from the trailer.
steps.solution: Stop, unload or reposition the hull, center it again, and secure it before driving.

steps.title: Tow Smoothly
steps.instruction: Accelerate gradually, brake early, and take wide turns. Sudden steering inputs can make the trailer swing or destabilize the load.
steps.largeBoatTip: Use wider corners and lower speed for heavy or long boats.
steps.problem: The trailer fishtails or clips roadside objects.
steps.solution: Release the accelerator, straighten the truck, and continue only after the trailer settles.

steps.title: Launch at the Ramp
steps.instruction: Reverse down the ramp in a straight line, use shallow corrections, and lower the trailer far enough for the boat to enter the water cleanly.
steps.largeBoatTip: Keep the truck aligned with the ramp so the hull does not rotate across the trailer.
steps.problem: The trailer catches the ramp or the boat launches sideways.
steps.solution: Pull forward, reset the approach, and reverse again with smaller steering movements.

steps.title: Recover and Park
steps.instruction: Center the trailer with the boat, load from a straight approach, secure the hull, and park where the workshop interaction is easy to reach.
steps.largeBoatTip: Use a slower final approach because long boats need more distance to settle on the trailer.
steps.problem: Physics leave the boat or trailer at an unstable angle.
steps.solution: Stop forcing the setup, detach when possible, reposition on level ground, and reconnect from a clean alignment.

flipABoatRebirthsAndUpdates.title: Flip A Boat Rebirths and Updates
flipABoatRebirthsAndUpdates.subtitle: Use rebirths to turn completed progression into lasting power, then track launch codes, fixes, boats, and feature updates from PolyCore Games.
flipABoatRebirthsAndUpdates.intro: Rebirth is the long-term progression system in Flip A Boat. Complete the target shown in the rebirth interface, confirm the reset, and begin a new flipping cycle with a permanent bonus that makes future progression stronger. Because the game launched with detailed repairs, large-boat progression, fleet storage, hauling, and realistic physics, update notes are especially useful for tracking fixes and balance changes.

updates.heading: Rebirth Requirement
updates.summary: Complete the progression target displayed in the in-game rebirth interface.
updates.content: Rebirth is designed for players who have already built cash through repeated boat flips. Open the rebirth screen, review the current target and bonus, and rebirth after the requirement is complete.

updates.heading: Reset and Permanent Bonus
updates.summary: A rebirth starts a new progression cycle and grants a lasting bonus for later runs.
updates.content: Use the permanent bonus to move through starter boats faster, rebuild cash, and return to larger projects. Rebirth is most valuable after the current run has reached its required milestone.

updates.heading: July 2026 — Public Launch
updates.summary: Flip A Boat launched with the full buy-repair-customize-sell loop.
updates.content: Launch features include broken boat purchases, part-by-part repairs, progression from a beat-up 11-foot boat to a 40-foot center console with quad outboards, realistic wave and boat physics, paint and customization, fleet storage, truck-and-trailer hauling, profitable resale, and rebirth bonuses.

updates.heading: July 2026 — Launch Code
updates.content: Open Settings from the bottom-left corner, scroll to the Codes section, enter release, and press Enter. The cash provides extra working capital for a boat purchase and required repair parts.

updates.heading: Launch Fixes and Balance Updates
updates.summary: PolyCore Games is actively fixing launch bugs and expanding the game.
updates.content: The Roblox description identifies the experience as a brand-new release and states that bugs will be fixed as quickly as possible. Check the Roblox experience page, PolyCore Games community, and official Discord for new boats, code drops, repair fixes, hauling changes, economy balance, and upcoming content.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Flip A Boat on Roblox

questions.question: What is Flip A Boat?
questions.answer: Flip A Boat is a Roblox boat restoration and reselling simulator from PolyCore Games. You buy damaged boats, repair every part, customize them, haul projects by truck and trailer, and resell restored boats for Cash and rebirth bonuses.

questions.question: How do I redeem Flip A Boat codes?
questions.answer: Open Flip A Boat on Roblox, tap the gear button in the bottom-left corner, scroll to the Redeem Code section in Settings, enter the code release, and press Enter to claim 10,000 Cash for your first boat and repair parts.

questions.question: What is the best beginner boat in Flip A Boat?
questions.answer: The best Flip A Boat beginner boat is the smallest project you can fully repair while keeping a Cash reserve for parts. Most new players start with the beat-up 11-foot starter boat, finish its required repairs, sell it, and reinvest the profit into the next tier.

questions.question: How does the Flip A Boat repair system work?
questions.answer: Flip A Boat uses a part-by-part repair system. Park the boat in the workshop, inspect every highlighted component, and purchase the exact parts the repair interface requests—outboard engines first, then steering, gauges, hull, and interior—before spending Cash on paint or customization.

questions.question: How do I make money in Flip A Boat?
questions.answer: To make money in Flip A Boat, buy a damaged boat below your total budget, reserve Cash for required parts, finish the functional repairs, limit cosmetic spending, then sell the restored boat for more than the purchase plus repair cost. Repeat profitable boat sizes before scaling up.

questions.question: What is rebirth in Flip A Boat?
questions.answer: Rebirth is Flip A Boat's long-term progression system. Once you complete the target shown in the rebirth interface, you reset the current cycle in exchange for a permanent bonus that makes every future run stronger, letting you rebuild cash and reach larger boats faster.
